rulings.Case Title: JERRY CHARLIFUE et al VS. ALLIED PACKING & SUPPLY, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 8, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Goodman Lumber Company's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Charlifue was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs' objections to defendant's Exhibits J, K, L and M are sustained. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' Exhibit B are overruled and its objections to plaintiffs' Exhibits E, G, H, I, J, K, L, M, N and O and their request for judicial notice are sustained. Plaintiffs' request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. section 437c(h) is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Charlifue was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' disputed material facts nos. 32 and 33 are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
